title: 10 best herbal creams for nipple pain during breastfeeding

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because it provides a soothing and calming effect on the sensitive skin of the nipples. The anti-inflammatory properties in the cream help to reduce swelling and discomfort, while its antioxidant compounds promote healing and tissue repair. As a result, the cream can provide immediate relief from soreness and tenderness, allowing mothers to continue breastfeeding comfortably and confidently.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula, also known as pot marigold, has anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and kaempferol that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the nipples, making it an effective natural remedy for breastfeeding mothers.

Antimicrobial properties: The antiseptic and antibacterial properties of calendula help prevent infections and promote wound healing by creating a protective barrier against bacteria and other microorganisms, reducing the risk of infection and promoting nipple health.

Soothing and emollient properties: Calendula's emollient and soothing qualities help to moisturize and calm irritated or damaged nipples, reducing pain, discomfort, and inflammation associated with breastfeeding difficulties.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing nipple pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Buds: The buds of the pot marigold plant are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers in full bloom and dry them thoroughly using a food dehydrator or desiccant at 135°f for 3 hours.
Combine 20g of dried calendula flowers with 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar and let it infuse for 4 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the infused oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and transfer the filtered oil to a double boiler.
Add 10g of beeswax pellets to the melted calendula oil in the double boiler and stir until fully incorporated then remove from heat and let it cool slightly.
Pour the cooled cream mixture into sterile glass containers filling them to the brim and seal tightly immediately refrigerating for a minimum of 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they provide soothing relief to tender nipples. The aloe vera gel, rich in vitamins and minerals, calms inflammation and reduces irritation caused by friction from nursing. It also hydrates the skin, softening and moisturizing the areola and nipple area, making it more comfortable for baby to latch on. As a result, mothers experience reduced pain and discomfort, allowing them to nurse with ease and confidence.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and inflammation on the nipples, providing relief from painful engorgement and soreness.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants neutralize free radicals, which can damage breast tissue and cause nipple pain, promoting healthy skin and reduced discomfort.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's gel accelerates wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and reducing bacterial growth, which helps to soothe cracked nipples and alleviate pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The thick, fleshy leaves of the aloe plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that help soothe and calm irritated nipples, reducing pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh aloe vera leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Blend the aloe vera leaves into a pulp using a juicer or blender for about 10 minutes.
Combine the aloe vera pulp with coconut oil and beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl weighing out approximately 1 part aloe vera pulp to 2 parts coconut oil and 1/4 part beeswax.
Heat the mixture over low heat stirring constantly for about 20 minutes or until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches a smooth consistency.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator at a temperature of 40°f or below for up to 3 weeks.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nipple soreness and pain caused by breastfeeding.

Antimicrobial properties: The herb's antimicrobial properties, including salicin and isorhapontigenin, help to prevent bacterial and fungal infections that can cause nipple pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.

Anesthetic and analgesic properties: Purple coneflower contains compounds like ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have been shown to have anesthetic and analgesic effects, helping to numb and relieve nipple pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming sore nipples.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, these compounds help to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and provide antioxidant benefits for sensitive skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers from a reputable source and sift through them to remove any debris.
Combine the echinacea petals with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar every other day to ensure even infusion.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 10 milliliters of beeswax and 2 drops of vitamin e oil to the infused oil and heat it gently in a double boiler until the mixture reaches 160 degrees fahrenheit.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream, containing echinacea, calms and cools the affected area, reducing swelling and discomfort. As a natural remedy, it effectively relieves soreness, burning, and stinging caused by latch issues or nipple thrush. By providing fast relief, herbal purple coneflower creams allow breastfeeding mothers to continue feeding their babies without interruption, promoting a positive experience for both mother and child.

Chickweed creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's natural ingredients, such as aloe vera and chamomile, work to calm and relax the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by breastfeeding. Additionally, chickweed's ability to reduce swelling and redness can help alleviate the painful effects of engorgement and nipple thrush. As a result, chickweed creams provide relief from nipple pain, allowing new mothers to enjoy a more comfortable and enjoyable breastfeeding experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chickweed cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the breast tissue, which can contribute to nipple soreness and pain.

Antibacterial and antifungal: The antimicrobial properties of chickweed cream prevent infections and promote healing by reducing the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause nipple thrush and other infections.

Emollient and soothing: Chickweed's emollient and soothing properties help moisturize and calm the nipples, providing relief from dryness, cracking, and irritation that can cause pain during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the breast tissue.
Buds: Contain high concentrations of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which help soothe and calm irritated breast tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stellaria media flowers and leaves according to sustainable foraging practices.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator at low temperature.
Mix one part dried stellaria media with two parts coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Add 10 drops of lavender essential oil and stir well to combine ingredients.
Store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The natural ingredients in plantain creams, such as aloe vera and tea tree oil, calm and comfort sore nipples, reducing redness and swelling. The creams also help to heal cracks and fissures caused by friction and dryness, promoting healthy skin and tissue. As a result, mothers can enjoy a more comfortable and stress-free breastfeeding experience for both themselves and their babies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain creams containing anti-inflammatory compounds, such as allantoin, can reduce swelling and redness caused by cracked or sore nipples, providing instant relief from discomfort.

Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of plantain, particularly berberine, can help prevent infection and promote healing by controlling the growth of bacteria that may exacerbate nipple pain and damage.

Soothing and calming: Plantain's natural soothing and calming effects can quickly ease nipple tenderness and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for nursing mothers experiencing sore or irritated nipples.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm nipple soreness, reducing discomfort for breastfeeding mothers.
Barks: The bark of the plantain plant contains a compound called aucubin, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in relieving nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried plantago major leaves measuring 10 grams per batch.
Combine the dried plantago major leaves with 20 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Allow the strained oil to mature for an additional week before using it as a cream by mixing it with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because it contains hyperforin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When applied topically to sore nipples, St John's Wort cream reduces inflammation and provides relief from pain caused by cracked or irritated skin. This allows new mothers to continue breastfeeding comfortably, promoting a healthy milk supply and bonding experience with their baby.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: St John's Wort cream reduces inflammation and swelling on the nipples, which can cause pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.

Antibacterial properties: St John's Wort cream has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent bacterial infections, which can cause nipple pain and thrush, a common condition in breastfeeding mothers.

Sedative and analgesic properties: St John's Wort cream contains sedative and analgesic compounds that help to numb the area and reduce nerve sensitivity, providing quick relief from nipple soreness and pain during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in hyperforin, a bioactive compound that helps reduce inflammation and promotes healing, making it effective in alleviating nipple pain during breastfeeding.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated nipples, reducing discomfort and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried st john's wort flowers hypericum perforatum, measuring 20g for 100ml cream preparation.
Combine harvested flowers with carrier oils coconut oil 30ml and olive oil 20ml in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture to a temperature of 60°c for 30 minutes allowing for gentle extraction of herbal compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours before use, resulting in a soothing nipple balm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because it contains allantoin, a natural soothing agent that accelerates wound healing and reduces inflammation. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties ease discomfort and irritation caused by friction or engorgement, allowing mothers to feed comfortably without distraction. Additionally, comfrey's moisturizing effects help to soften and protect the skin, further alleviating pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey's allantoin and rosmarinic acid components reduce inflammation, swelling, and redness, providing relief from painful nipples.
Wound healing properties: Comfrey's glycosides and flavonoids accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, cell growth, and tissue repair, helping to soothe and protect sore nipples.
Soothing and calming effects: Comfrey's triterpenoid saponins have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety, stress, and discomfort associated with nipple pain during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps soothe and calm irritated nipples.
Leaves: High in rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation and promotes healing in the breast tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest symphytum officinale leaves by selecting fresh healthy plants and carefully cutting off entire leaves for use.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours or until completely dry.
Mix one part dried symphytum officinale leaves with three parts coconut oil in a double boiler, heating mixture over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding solids and storing cream in the refrigerator at 39-45 degrees fahrenheit.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken completely before use, which may take several hours or overnight depending on storage conditions and desired consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues. Nettle's natural antihistamine properties also help to alleviate inflammation and itching, providing instant relief for sore nipples. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects can help to hydrate and nourish the skin, promoting healthy tissue growth and healing. As a result, stinging nettle creams provide effective and gentle relief from nipple pain, allowing breastfeeding mothers to feed comfortably and bond with their babies without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the breast tissue, which may contribute to nipple pain during breastfeeding.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle cream can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate nipple soreness and discomfort during breastfeeding.

Hormone-regulating properties: Stinging nettle has been shown to have a regulatory effect on hormone levels, including prolactin, which is essential for lactation. This may help alleviate nipple pain caused by hormonal imbalances or fluctuations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing nipple pain.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to calm irritated skin and reduce pain associated with nipple thrush.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried stinging nettle leaves and flowers with 100 milliliters of organic coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours at room temperature before straining through cheesecloth to remove solids.
Whip the infused oil into a creamy consistency using a hand blender and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild strawberry creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its unique properties that soothe and calm irritated nipples. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds present in wild strawberries, such as salicylic acid and ellagic acid, help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. Additionally, the creams' moisturizing effects can alleviate dryness and cracking caused by frequent nursing, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable breastfeeding experience overall.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild strawberry creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in sore nipples, providing relief from pain.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties can help prevent infections that may cause nipple pain by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi, promoting a healthy breast tissue environment.

Emollient and soothing properties: Wild strawberry creams typically contain moisturizing ingredients like glycerin and lanolin, which can help soften and calm irritated skin, reducing discomfort and soreness associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are believed to soothe and calm irritated nipples.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, seeds provide moisturizing properties that help alleviate dryness and discomfort associated with nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fragaria vesca leaves and flowers, drying them for 2-3 hours in a warm oven at 150°f to preserve potency.
Infuse dried herbs in a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil for 4-6 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids and retaining 1 cup of herbal oil.
Mix the herbal oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax, melting them together in a double boiler at 180°f for 10-15 minutes.
Pour the creamy mixture into sterilized jars, sealing them tightly and allowing the cream to cool and solidify within 30 minutes before storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's natural ingredients, such as dandelion extract, chamomile, and calendula, work together to reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by nipple soreness. As a result, the cream provides instant relief and promotes healing, allowing mothers to continue breastfeeding comfortably and confidently.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and discomfort in the nipple area, alleviating pain associated with mastitis or nipple thrush.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial components of dandelion cream, including triterpenoid saponins and phenolic acids, combat bacterial or fungal infections that can cause nipple pain and inflammation, promoting a healthy breast environment.

Soothing and emollient properties: Dandelion's moisturizing and nourishing compounds, such as vitamins A, C, and E, and fatty acids, help soothe and hydrate the nipple area, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by friction or chafing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots can help reduce inflammation and soothe nipple pain caused by breastfeeding.
Fruits: The berries or fruits of the dandelion plant contain vitamins and minerals that promote wound healing and skin regeneration, making them beneficial for soothing nipple discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves according to local regulations and ensure they are free of contaminants.
Combine one part dried or one cup fresh dandelion flowers and leaves with four parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to infuse in a cool dark place for two weeks shaking daily to facilitate extraction of active compounds.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding solids and retaining 250 milliliters of liquid extract.
Mix 50 milliliters of the herbal liquid extract with one tablespoon beeswax and one teaspoon sweet almond oil then whip until smooth to create a soothing cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they provide a soothing and calming effect on the nipple tissue. The anti-inflammatory properties of lavender help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by friction or latch issues. Additionally, the topical application of lavender cream can also aid in promoting wound healing and reducing the risk of nipple trauma, allowing mothers to feed comfortably and enjoy the bonding experience with their babies.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Helps to relax the muscle fibers surrounding the nipples, reducing spasms and discomfort caused by latch issues or poor latching.
Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces swelling and redness in the nipple area, which can occur as a result of frequent feeding, irritation from baby's tongue or mouth, or other causes.
Anxiolytic property: Calms nervous tension and promotes relaxation, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate nipple pain during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of essential oils, which provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing nipple pain.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, two compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavender flowers measuring 2 tablespoons and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered lavender with sweet almond oil in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes to create an infused oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard solids retaining 4 ounces of oil.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax pellets measuring 2 tablespoons and cocoa butter measuring 1 tablespoon in a double boiler at 180°f for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass containers and let it cool to room temperature before use taking approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because its soothing properties calm and comfort sensitive nipples. The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm reduce swelling and ease discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for sore nipples. Additionally, lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the breast, allowing for smoother milk flow and reduced nipple pain. This results in a more comfortable breastfeeding experience for both mom and baby.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax and soothe sore and irritated nipples, reducing muscle spasms and discomfort caused by breastfeeding.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm creams can reduce swelling and redness of the nipples, making them feel softer and more comfortable during feeding sessions.
Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) property: Lemon balm's calming effects can help to alleviate stress and anxiety related to nipple pain, promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness for both mom and baby.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain rosmarinic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help soothe and relieve nipple pain during breastfeeding.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have been traditionally used to treat nipple soreness due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce discomfort and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis flowers and grind into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix ground melissa officinalis flowers with sweet almond oil at a ratio of 10:1 for optimal potency.
Combine the mixture in a double boiler and heat gently over low heat for 30 minutes to infuse oil with herbal properties.
Remove from heat and let cool, then strain through cheesecloth into a clean glass container for storage.
Whip cream by mixing cooled infused oil with beeswax at a ratio of 20:1 and heating until melted, then pouring into prepared containers for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as salicylic acid and rosin, help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the nipple area, making it a soothing solution for nursing mothers. As a result, the nipple pain associated with breastfeeding is alleviated, allowing mothers to continue feeding their babies comfortably and without disruption.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The plant contains flavonoids and polyphenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which could help reduce swelling and discomfort in the nipples.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Joe Pye Weed has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacteria and fungi, which could help prevent infections that may contribute to nipple pain during breastfeeding.
Wound-healing compounds: The plant contains triterpenoids and lignans with potential wound-healing and tissue-regenerating properties, which could aid in the healing of sore or cracked nipples.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing redness and discomfort caused by nipple soreness.
Barks: The barks contain compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from nipple pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the herb into small pieces and combine with 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Allow the mixture to infuse for at least 2 hours in a cool dark place before straining and discarding solids.
Mix the infused oil with 10% beeswax and heat until melted then whip until smooth and creamy consistency is achieved.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated nipples. The cream's natural ingredients, such as hawthorn berries, richly nourish and protect the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, hawthorn's antispasmodic effects help to relax the breast tissue, easing tension and pain caused by engorgement or poor latching. Anti-inflammatory Compounds: Hawthorn's flavonoids and polyphenols have anti-inflammatory effects that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness, swelling, and pain associated with nipple thrush or other forms of nipple pain.

Buds: Contain flavonoids and anthocyanins that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried crataegus monogyna flowers and 10ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Combine the dried herbs in a small bowl and carefully pour the carrier oil over them, allowing for a 1:5 ratio of herb to oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over simmering water for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, pressing on the solids to extract as much oil as possible.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Agrimony creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they provide a soothing, natural barrier that cushions the nipples and reduces friction caused by infant suction. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to calm any swelling or redness that can occur after feeding. Additionally, agrimony has been shown to promote healthy wound healing, which can aid in the recovery of cracked or sore nipples often experienced during breastfeeding.

remedy_recipe: Harvest agrimonia eupatoria leaves and flowers, ensuring fresh and clean materials for optimal potency.
Combine 30g of dried agrimonia eupatoria with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 hours under low heat, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the oil.

Mix the infused oil with 10% beeswax and 1% vitamin e oil, heating the blend gently until the wax is fully incorporated and the cream has a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they contain astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the affected area. remedy_recipe: Harvest galium aparine roots and dried them according to traditional methods for at least six months.
Grind 20 grams of dried galium aparine root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered root with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat it gently over low heat for two hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any remaining plant material and discard the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to an airtight container and storing it in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterbur creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they contain a natural ingredient called petasins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petasites hybridus leaves and chop them into small pieces, ensuring you have 100g of dried material for the recipe.
Combine the chopped petasites hybridus with 500ml of olive oil in a glass jar, allowing it to infuse for 4 weeks under sunlight.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the herb residue, retaining 250ml of infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 120°f.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized jars, allowing it to solidify before use within 6 weeks and storing any leftover cream in an airtight container for up to 3 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they provide a soothing and calming effect on the nipple tissue. The anti-inflammatory properties of cornflower extract help to reduce swelling and redness, while its analgesic properties ease discomfort and pain caused by nipple irritation or thrush. Additionally, cornflower's natural moisturizing properties help to keep the nipples soft and hydrated, making it an effective remedy for addressing nipple pain and promoting comfortable breastfeeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cornflower contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling and pain in the nipples.
Antibacterial properties: The herb's antimicrobial compounds help prevent bacterial infections that can cause nipple pain and discomfort.
Soothing and calming effects: Cornflower has been traditionally used as a topical treatment for skin irritations, including nipple soreness, due to its ability to calm and soothe irritated tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cornflower roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated nipples.
Barks: Cornflower barks have astringent properties that can help reduce swelling and ease nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried centaurea cyanus flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 2 tablespoons of coconut oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of ground centaurea cyanus to the melted mixture and stir well to combine, allowing it to steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids and allowing the cream to cool completely.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized jars, sealing them tightly and storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, ready to use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated nipples. The damask rose extract reduces redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with nipple soreness, allowing mothers to feed their babies comfortably. The cream's moisturizing effects also help to hydrate and protect the nipple tissue, promoting healing and reducing pain caused by friction or irritation. This natural remedy provides relief for mothers, making breastfeeding a more enjoyable experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Damask rose cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nipples, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with breastfeeding.
Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The antimicrobial properties of damask rose cream help prevent bacterial and fungal infections that can cause nipple pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.
Emollient and soothing properties: The moisturizing and emollient properties of damask rose cream soften and soothe irritated nipples, reducing friction and discomfort caused by breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated nipples.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that provide natural analgesic and antispasmodic properties, relieving nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried rosa damascena flowers for infusion.
Combine 20g of dried rosa damascena flowers with 500ml boiling water for 10-15 minutes steeping.
Strain the infused liquid and discard solids, then mix with 100g beeswax and 50g coconut oil.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to ensure thorough emulsification.
Pour the final cream into sterilized containers, allowing it to cool and set before storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its soothing and calming properties. The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, work to reduce swelling and discomfort caused by nipple trauma. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects help to soften and nourish the skin, allowing for a more comfortable feeding experience. As a result, breastfeeding mothers can enjoy a more relaxed and enjoyable feeding routine.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness, which can alleviate nipple tenderness and pain caused by friction or engorgement.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic compounds relax the muscles in the breast tissue and surrounding skin, reducing muscle spasms that can cause nipple pain and discomfort.
Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's soothing and calming properties promote relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to nipple pain during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing nipple pain caused by inflammation or irritation during breastfeeding.
Roots: Chamomile roots contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to calm and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from nipple pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler at medium heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the mixture and stir until melted and fully incorporated taking approximately 5-7 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer until light and fluffy taking about 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream to a sterilized container and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage allowing the cream to fully solidify at room temperature before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for nipple pain during breastfeeding are topical treatments made from natural ingredients, such as herbs and plant extracts, specifically designed to soothe and calm sore nipples. These creams work by providing a barrier between the nipple and breast milk, reducing friction and discomfort caused by latch issues or other factors. Examples of herbal creams that help with nipple pain include those containing calendula, chamomile, and tea tree oil. By using these creams, breastfeeding mothers can experience significant relief from pain, allowing them to continue feeding comfortably and confidently, ultimately improving their overall breastfeeding experience and bonding with their baby.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nipple pain during breastfeeding with herbal creams is by using a topical application of calendula cream. Calendula has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated nipples, reducing pain and discomfort. Apply the cream to the affected area after feeding, gently massaging it in until fully absorbed. This natural remedy provides relief without any adverse effects on baby or breastmilk.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nipple pain during breastfeeding that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile can provide relief from inflammation, itching, and discomfort associated with these conditions. These creams can help soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting healing and reducing pain, making them a popular natural alternative for treating various skin irritations.
